about.title: # about.md
lines: Hello! I am a Computer Science student at the University of Washington - Seattle. I love learning about algorithms and how to make programs efficient. I am currently on a journey to becoming a proficient infrastructure engineer, primarily using C++ through my ongoing systems engineering projects.

What first drew me into programming was game development. Growing up playing video games sparked my curiosity about how they were created. However, it wasn't until university that I finally began to explore this long-standing interest.

projects.title: # projects.md
lines: A 20-month journey through systems programming, progressing from fundamental data structures to networking protocols. Building systems from first principles to develop deep understanding of memory management, concurrency, and hardware interaction.
**Tech Stack:** C++17, C++11, GCC, GDB, Valgrind, GoogleTest, Raspberry Pi 5
- Dynamic Array, Singly/Doubly Linked Lists, Stack, Queue
- Red-Black Tree, Bloom Filter, Ring Buffer, Skip List
- CRC32/Internet Checksum, Token/Leaky Bucket rate limiters
*Using C++11 for deeper POSIX API understanding*
- UDP from Scratch - Complete UDP protocol
- TCP from Scratch - Full implementation with congestion control

Operating systems partner project building a Unix-like kernel in C for CSE 451. Implemented core OS subsystems from scratch including a crash-safe file system, virtual memory, and hardened concurrency primitives.
- Write-ahead logging and crash recovery — dirty blocks staged atomically to a log region before commit, replayed on boot via apply_log, validated against automated crash injection
- Extent-based file write engine over a 31-extent inode array with block coalescing, logical-to-physical translation, and transaction-safe chunking to prevent log overflow on concurrent writes
- Demand-paging stack growth in the page fault handler and sys_sbrk heap extension, fixing single-page allocation bugs in both paths
- Lock assertions and concurrency hardening to inode persistence, eliminating a class of race conditions on write_dinode

POSIX-compliant shell built from scratch in C++ as part of the CodeCrafters "Build Your Own Shell" challenge. Implements a full REPL with command parsing, built-in commands, and external program execution.

Multi-threaded HTTP/1.1 web server in C++ from scratch for CSE 333. Utilizes a thread pool to manage concurrent client connections over IPv4/IPv6. Includes a full-text search engine in C with a file-based inverted index built from 10,000+ documents and a multi-index query processor with ranked results.
**Tech Stack:** C++, C, HTTP/1.1, IPv4/IPv6, Threading
- Multi-index query processor with ranked results
Simple tool for Gradescope that shows assignments due soon and how many days until they are due. Forked a gradescope-api and added the web part so that people can see their assignments easier.
- Web interface for easier assignment viewing
Class project e-commerce platform with product management and SQLite3 database integration.
**Tech Stack:** JavaScript, SQL, HTML, CSS, SQLite3

hobbies.title: # hobbies.md
lines: I was a competitive swimmer starting at age 11 and continued through high school. Although I stopped competing when I entered college, I still enjoy swimming recreationally from time to time.
I was more serious about lifting in high school, but I still enjoy getting a good workout in. There's something satisfying about pushing yourself in the gym.
I love learning about new gym supplements and trying them out. I find the science behind supplementation fascinating and enjoy experimenting with what works best for my body.
I'm a huge fan of atmospheric horror games. Some of my favorites include Dead Space, Silent Hill 2, and Resident Evil 7. I love the tension and storytelling that horror games deliver.
I also enjoy Japanese role-playing games, particularly the Persona series and Final Fantasy. The combination of engaging narratives, character development, and strategic combat really draws me in.

I enjoy investing in the stock market and find learning about financial markets, economic trends, and investment strategies to be incredibly fascinating. It's a continuous learning process that I find both challenging and rewarding.
My favorite dog breed is the **Shiba Inu**!

technicalSkills.title: # technical-skills.md
lines: C++ 17, C 11, Java 11, Python 3.13, SQL, JavaScript, TypeScript,
React, Node.js, Flask, REST APIs, API Design
Git, Version Control, Visual Studio Code, neovim, Terminal, Linux/Windows Environment

extras.title: # extras.md
lines: **std::variant** - Type-safe union that holds one of several types
**std::optional** - Represents optional values without null pointers
**Fold Expressions** - Compact syntax for variadic template operations
**Cache Line Interface** - Hardware constructive/destructive interference sizes for cache-aware programming
- The Art of Writing Efficient Programs (Pikus)
